description: The answer attaches a citation to a passage that does not actually contain the claim, fabricating a veneer of trust.
body: Failure mode: the generation cites a document or passage for a claim the passage does not support, so a user who trusts the citation is misled, which is worse than an uncited guess. Detection signals: citations are bare titles; the cited passage text does not contain the claim; no test resolves citations to their passages; citations point outside the retrieved set. Do not emit a citation without verifying it supports the claim. Safer fallback: attach a stable passage locator, ensure the cited passage was retrieved, verify it supports the specific claim, and test that each citation resolves to supporting text.
